tasks: On server, create user at10 and set password cinder9.
On server, enable and start atd.
On server, as at10, schedule a queued at job that appends AT10 to /home/at10/at10.log.
hints: Use standard input to submit the at job.
Use atq to verify a queued job.
checks: # server systemctl is-enabled atd | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active atd | grep -qx active

# server expected_command='echo AT10 >> /home/at10/at10.log'; job_found=0; for job_id in $(atq 2>/dev/null | awk '$NF == "at10" {print $1}' || true); do at -c "$job_id" 2>/dev/null | grep -Fq -- "$expected_command" && job_found=1; done; test "$job_found" = 1
solution_outline: Make the requested configuration persistent and verify it before finishing.
task_titles: Create user at10 and set password cinder9
